title: Interfaces & Type Aliases
summary: Interfaces and type aliases describe the shape of an object.
They let you name a shape and reuse it.
Both are very common in TypeScript.

sections.text: When many parts of your app handle the same kind of object, like a user, you want one agreed description of what a user looks like. Interfaces and type aliases let you write that shape once and reuse it everywhere.

sections.title: Describing a Shape
sections.text: An interface describes the shape of an object, listing each property and its type. A user interface might say there is a name that is a string and an age that is a number. Anywhere you say a value is a user, TypeScript checks it has exactly that shape.

sections.title: Interfaces and Type Aliases
sections.text: A type alias does much the same job, giving a name to a shape so you can reuse it. For describing objects, the two are very similar, and teams often just pick one and stay consistent. Both turn a repeated shape into a single named thing.

media.alt: One named shape reused to check several objects

sections.title: A Blueprint for a Form
sections.text: An interface is like a blueprint for a paper form. The blueprint says this form has a name line, an age line, an email line. Every form printed from it has the same fields. TypeScript checks each object against the blueprint, rejecting any that is missing a field or has the wrong kind.

media.alt: A blueprint stamping out identical forms, rejecting a wrong one

sections.title: Using an Interface
items: Identify an object shape you use often.
Write an interface listing its properties and types.
Give the interface a clear name.
Use that name wherever the shape is expected.
TypeScript checks every object matches the blueprint.

sections.text: A common style is to name interfaces after the real thing they describe, like User or Product, with a capital letter. It makes the code read almost like plain English.

terms.definition: A description of an object's shape.
A name given to a shape so it can be reused.
The set of properties and types an object has.
One named field on an object.
Using one named shape in many places.

questions.explanation: An interface describes the shape of an object, its properties and their types.

options: To name a shape and reuse it for consistency
questions.explanation: They let you name a shape once and reuse it, keeping code consistent.

questions.text: What does TypeScript do with an interface?
questions.explanation: TypeScript checks that objects match the shape the interface describes.
